episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: A lot of times they were moving around but not doing anything useful.

feedbackItems.reason: While the pink chef is grabbing more onions and the green chef is waiting for the soup to be done, the blue and orange chefs are just running around aimlessly when they could be preparing another pot of soup instead.
feedbackItems.feedback: The blue and orange chefs are not being helpful.

feedbackItems.reason: There are a lot of moments where chefs will pause and just walk around for a second instead of grabbing more onions to put in the other pot.
feedbackItems.feedback: It seems like overall, there is very poor collaboration as everyone is not moving together smoothly to get the job done.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: I did not have any trouble comprehending what they were doing.

feedbackItems.reason: I am not understanding why the chefs are ignoring the other pot.
feedbackItems.feedback: The blue chef is holding the onion and waiting for the other chef to be done with that soup, when he could be using the other pot to make more soup.

feedbackItems.reason: He did not even grab an onion or do anything to help the process. He instead just stayed there for a few moments.
feedbackItems.feedback: The green chef is not doing anything here.
I like that they are moving around in a circle instead of bumping into one another.
feedbackItems.reason: In the previous video, it felt like the chefs were awkward in their movements, but it seems like in this one they have a smooth system.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: I did not have any problems monitoring the agents.

feedbackItems.feedback: The chefs on the left seem to clash into one another, a problem that could be easily solved if the chefs would pay attention to the other pot on the right and use that to cook more soup.
feedbackItems.reason: Like I said before, I do not understand why the chefs are ignoring the pot on the right.

feedbackItems.feedback: I like that overall the chefs each seem to have their own job to do, ensuring smoother communication. But I think it seems like the green and purple chefs are crashing into each other too often, a problem that could be solved by using the pot on the right.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: I could easily monitor both agents.

feedbackItems.feedback: I feel like out of all the videos so far, this one shows the best collaboration as there is nobody bumping into each other or standing and doing nothing.

surveyAnswers.q1: If there were several chefs it was harder for me to monitor them all at once, so I preferred the videos with fewer agents.
surveyAnswers.q2: I cannot think of anything that would have helped me other than having fewer agents in the videos.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Neither moved chaotically or randomly.

feedbackItems.reason: It reduced efficiency.
feedbackItems.feedback: Instead of standing around waiting, Chef Blue could help add onions to the other pot.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Blue seemed aware of what Green was doing and did a good job efficiently delivering across the closed line.

feedbackItems.reason: It increased efficiency.
feedbackItems.feedback: It was good that chef blue prepared another onion on his way to pick up the plate, so chef green could serve the dish and immediately begin making another pot.

feedbackItems.reason: It reduced efficiency.

episodes.monitoringDifficultyReason: Watching four chefs do four different things at once, all interacting with each other, took some pausing and repeat viewing, but it wasn't impossible or extremely difficult.

feedbackItems.feedback: Blue is being very efficient, helping serve onions to the chefs with the pots and then moving on to provide plates. Orange however is being redundant, basically following blue around. They could have divided the two tasks between them.
feedbackItems.reason: One chef is being very efficient and one is being very inefficient.

feedbackItems.feedback: Green and pink are doing an excellent job working as a team to get both pots going, but neither is focused on delivering the final output, only one dish got served in the whole video.
feedbackItems.reason: The bots are accomplishing one task but not seeing the "big picture"
It reduced efficiency.

surveyAnswers.q2: N/A - the frame by frame and ability to pause and rewind made it much easier than it would have been otherwise.
surveyAnswers.q1: It was especially difficult to keep track of all four independent agents when the kitchen was divided and each should have been doing a different task, although sometimes they just wandered or followed each other around.
surveyAnswers.q3: No issues, questions, comments, or concerns, thank you!

they worked pretty well together considering there were twice as many chefs this round. they have a second stove and they would walk to the empty stove while the other stove was in use, yet no one took the initiative to cook on the second stove to make meals quicker.
feedbackItems.reason: while they were productive and worked well togehter, they missed the oppurtunity to use the second stove and double their cooking rate
i didn't feel they were being the most efficient with their time
